ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general sushi class (FOODON_03311737); no specific gunkan or capelin roe sushi subclass exists. Mapped to the closest parent sushi class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ covers sushi as a category. No specific gunkan maki or masago subcode identified in the public FoodEx2 catalogue; closest parent sushi code used as the defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for gunkan sushi or capelin roe sushi as of 2024.

allergens: gluten (nori wrapping may contain traces; soy sauce seasoning contains wheat)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Capelin Roe Gunkan Sushi is 4 pieces (approximately 100 g), each piece consisting of a hand-formed ball of vinegared sushi rice wrapped with a strip of nori and topped with seasoned capelin roe (masago).

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Sushi, with fish, 4 pieces (composite); supplemented with capelin roe (masago) nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy entry for fish roe
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for capelin roe gunkan sushi as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central sushi composite entries (FDC ID 174237 — Sushi, with fish) and USDA SR Legacy fish roe entry (FDC ID 175168 — Fish, roe, mixed species, raw), weighted to reflect the approximate composition of gunkan sushi (approximately 70% sushi rice, 25% capelin roe, 5% nori). Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA composite and retained where consistent. Energy 150 kcal/100g, protein 8.5g, fat 3.5g, carbohydrate 21g are consistent with the USDA sushi-with-fish composite range and are accepted. Sodium 350 mg/100g is plausible given seasoned masago and sushi rice vinegar. All values documented as adapted/estimated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for sushi rice / white rice sushi
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for capelin roe gunkan sushi as a composite dish. GI estimated at 55 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sushi made with white short-grain rice (GI approximately 48–55, reference glucose). The dominant carbohydrate source is vinegared sushi rice (~70% of dish weight); capelin roe and nori contribute negligible carbohydrate. Per standard GI classification thresholds (low: ≤55, medium: 56–69, high: ≥70), GI 55 falls in the low category. Glycemic load per 100g serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g) / 100 = (55 × 21) / 100 = 11.55, rounded to 11.6. Estimation basis documented per Veritas source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 350 mg per 100 g from seasoned masago and sushi rice vinegar, with additional sodium load from soy sauce dipping
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit capelin roe gunkan sushi to 1–2 pieces per meal, strictly avoid or replace soy sauce dipping with a low-sodium alternative, and account for the cumulative sodium from masago seasoning and rice vinegar within their daily 1500–2000 mg sodium target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined short-grain sushi rice at 21 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1 g dietary fibre, yielding a low GI of approximately 55 and a medium glycemic load of 11.6 per 100 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to 1–2 pieces per occasion, pair with high-fibre or protein-rich foods to blunt the postprandial glucose response, and request brown rice sushi where available to reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 350 mg per 100 g and cholesterol 70 mg per 100 g from capelin roe; however, omega-3 fatty acids in masago provide a cardioprotective offset and saturated fat is low at 1 g per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume capelin roe gunkan sushi in moderation (1–2 pieces), benefiting from the omega-3 content of masago, but must strictly limit soy sauce dipping to control sodium intake and monitor total cholesterol contribution from roe across the meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Capelin roe is a source of omega-3 fatty acids with anti-inflammatory properties; saturated fat is low at 1 g per 100 g and trans fat is zero
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Capelin roe gunkan sushi is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the anti-inflammatory omega-3 content of masago and the antioxidant contribution of nori; soy sauce dipping should be minimised to avoid sodium-driven tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Capelin Roe Gunkan Sushi (軍艦巻き with masago) is a Japanese sushi variety consisting of four hand-formed balls of vinegared short-grain sushi rice, each wrapped with a vertical strip of nori seaweed to form a boat-shaped cup (gunkan, meaning 'warship'), filled with seasoned capelin roe (masago). Masago is the small, bright orange roe of the capelin fish (Mallotus villosus), prized in Japanese cuisine for its mild, slightly briny flavour, delicate crunch, and vibrant colour. The dish is assembled rather than cooked, with the rice seasoned with rice vinegar, sugar, and salt, and the roe typically seasoned with soy sauce and mirin. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 150 kcal, 8.5 g protein, 3.5 g fat (of which 1 g is saturated), 21 g carbohydrate, 1 g dietary fibre, and 350 mg sodium. The dominant carbohydrate source is the sushi rice, which has a low glycemic index of approximately 55, making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Capelin roe contributes omega-3 fatty acids, iodine, and approximately 70 mg cholesterol per 100 g. Clinically, the primary concerns are sodium load (especially when combined with soy sauce dipping) for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients, and the refined carbohydrate content for Type 2 Diabetes management. The omega-3 content of masago provides anti-inflammatory benefit relevant to rheumatoid arthritis. This dish is commonly served in Japanese restaurants and sushi conveyor-belt establishments across Singapore and Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for capelin roe gunkan sushi as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central sushi-with-fish composite and USDA SR Legacy fish roe entries, cross-checked against the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). Values are internally consistent and plausible but represent an adapted estimate rather than a direct measurement. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white rice sushi. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for capelin roe gunkan sushi as a composite dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or the Singapore HPB database as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not reported in the adapted USDA source entries used. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite dish are not available in the adapted USDA source entries as of 2024. 4. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero): capelin roe and sushi rice are natural, minimally processed foods with no industrial trans fat; this is consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for fish roe and cooked short-grain rice. 5. Nutrient values are adapted estimates; a direct laboratory analysis of this composite dish would be required for high-confidence classification. 6. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was not used as a primary nutrient evidence source as it is not on the approved source list; USDA FoodData Central was used instead. 7. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for white rice sushi; no direct GI measurement for capelin roe gunkan sushi was found in approved sources. Per standard GI classification thresholds (low: ≤55, medium: 56–69, high: ≥70), GI 55 is classified as low. 8. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2, as the matched codes represent parent/broader sushi categories rather than exact matches for gunkan sushi with capelin roe.
